what_it_does: Acceptance test that proves the live UI registry serves element-level addresses from the design corpus, preserves the original bare region handles, and verifies that every data-ui-ref used by the live app has a registry entry.
observations: Builds a real Suite over an isolated temporary FsStore, explicitly avoiding the live ~/company/.data store.
Loads design/_system/addresses.json and asserts at least 24 element-level addresses are present in the served registry.
Validates every served corpus address against parse_ui_address and UnionAddressRecord.
Checks capability normalization, e.g. driven-read-only becomes drivenReadOnly.
Uses a regex scanner that captures both plain quoted and JSX brace-expression data-ui-ref attributes.
Includes inline regression-guard cases for the scanner to ensure the brace-form blind spot stays closed.
Excludes run:// live node addresses from the orphan check.
Separates live app refs from redesign mockup refs and treats mockup-only proposed addresses as informational, not failures.
Preserves and verifies the original seven bare region handles plus the '*' canvas handle.
standouts: The scanner regression guard explicitly tests four carrier forms, including JSX brace expressions, to prevent a known false-green.
The policy that mockup-proposed addresses are illustrative future surfaces and must not gate the live registry.
The test runs against the real Suite/ui_info path over an isolated store rather than mocking the registry.
conventions: Docstring states the IS / SHOULD-BE / verify-by-use contract before the code.
Uses a global PASS counter and a small check() helper for each acceptance criterion.
Reads the design corpus as JSON and walks source directories for static attribute scanning.
Hard-codes the original seven bare handles and the '*' canvas handle for preservation checks.
concerns: collect_refs reads whole files into memory and regex-scans them, so it can miss non-literal or dynamically constructed refs.
The first failing check() aborts the whole test because it uses assert, giving no partial report.
The test is tightly coupled to specific corpus size (>=24) and handle names that may drift.
The regex may still miss template-literal backtick forms or other JSX expression patterns.
It accesses Suite.UI_REGISTRY and Suite._registry_ui_target, which appear to be internal attributes.
notes: A backward-compatible acceptance test for the S1 registry migration that guards both semantic coverage and the scanner used to prove it.
questions: How do NodeRegistry.discover and Suite.ui_info actually assemble the merged bare + full address set?
What is the exact schema returned by UnionAddressRecord.from_corpus and validate_address_record?
Where are run:// refs generated and why are they considered live node addresses rather than registry entries?
What is fe-map §8 and how did the Studio redesign corpus land on main?
How does build_ui_info prevent duplicate-key collisions between bare handles and full ui:// strings?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it proves S1 element-level registry coverage, zero live-app orphans, and preservation of bare handles.
apparent_themes: Additive migration without breaking existing consumers
Canonical address spine derived from design corpus
Regression guarding for static-analysis blind spots
Strict isolation of acceptance tests from production data
Distinction between live runtime addresses, canonical registry entries, and future mockup proposals
intention_signals: To make the design corpus the source of truth for UI addresses while guaranteeing existing bare-handle consumers keep working and no live DOM reference is left unregistered.
novelty: The explicit brace-form scanner regression guard and the rule that mockup-only proposed addresses are reported but not gated.
contribution: A runnable acceptance contract that encodes the S1 registry invariant: corpus addresses served, live app refs registered, bare handles preserved, and scanner robustness regression-tested.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test that validates the live UI registry serves design-corpus element addresses, preserves legacy bare region handles, and ensures zero live-app data-ui-ref orphans.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the S1 registry migration is additive and complete by exercising the real Suite over an isolated store.

symbols.does: Asserts a labeled condition and increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: The check(label, cond) helper is used throughout the acceptance test to gate each criterion; it aborts on the first failure via assert and prints an ok line for every passing check.

symbols.does: Recursively scans source files and extracts static data-ui-ref attribute values.
symbols.description: collect_refs(root_dir, exts) walks the given directory, reads files with the specified extensions, applies the _REF regex to capture quoted and JSX brace-quoted data-ui-ref values, drops template-literal interpolations containing ${, and returns the set of distinct static refs.
